pipelines.name: build-mvp
pipelines.description: Bootstrap a playable game from a blank slate. Model picks the genre in step 1, writes a design doc in step 2, then scaffolds + builds the core loop in steps 3-5, and audits the MVP in step 6. Each step waits for the previous step's PR to merge before starting, so step N's worktree sees step N-1's landed code.
pipelines.goal_signal: src/ contains a playable game (npm test passes, `node src/main.ts` or equivalent runs to a terminal/render state without crashing)

steps.description: Read CLAUDE.md constraints (TypeScript, playable, not RPG/sand). Pick ONE specific game genre + core mechanic that fits. Write rationale to ai/DECISIONS.md including: chosen genre, why it fits constraints, one-paragraph core-loop description, two example MVP features.

Read ai/DECISIONS.md. Write a short Game Design Document (GDD) at ai/GDD.md: sections for Core Loop, Win/Loss, Player Actions, Minimum Playable Scope (5-7 concrete features), and Out-of-Scope-for-MVP. Keep it tight — under 200 lines.

SCAFFOLD: initialize package.json (declare vitest + typescript devDeps but do NOT run npm install), tsconfig.json, src/main.ts with a stub entry point, src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts with one passing test, .gitignore. Update ai/STATE.md with a Next: line that points at the first GDD feature.

FIRST FEATURE: implement the smallest GDD feature (e.g. the player's primary action: take input, return a result). Add unit tests in src/__tests__/. Update ai/STATE.md Next: line for the following feature.

CORE LOOP: wire input → update → render. Keep render as plaintext stdout for MVP. The game must be runnable end-to-end via `node src/main.ts` (or via a `start` script). Add a loop-level test in src/__tests__/. Update ai/STATE.md.
Audit the MVP. Verify: tests pass, game runs end-to-end, code aligns with GDD scope, no dead files. Write findings to ai/AUDIT-mvp.md. Critical findings will abort the next pipeline.
